page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores operating at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 11 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong processing and memory capabilities. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2176.0 and a multi-core score of 19330.0. Memory bandwidth tests show strong read-only performance, reaching 3838.93 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 193.31 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, though large block write bandwidth is average at 77.53 GB/sec. The CPU architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and AMX, which contribute to strong LLM inference speeds, such as 348.34 tokens/sec for small model text generation and 29.72 tokens/sec for medium model text generation.

The c8i-flex.12xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory profile within the compute-optimized class, utilizing DDR5 memory to offset the lack of local storage. Its hardware configuration is tailored for workloads requiring high integer and floating-point performance without the overhead of local NVMe drives. Based on benchmark data, this instance is suited for tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers strong benchmark performance, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2176.0 and a multi-core score of 19330.0. It demonstrates top-tier capabilities in static web serving and image processing, alongside strong memory read bandwidth of up to 3838.93 GB/sec. Supported by AVX-512 and AMX extensions, it is suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong performance for compilation and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring forty-eight Intel Xeon vCPUs and ninety-six gigabytes of high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
11 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Strong performance in software compilation, database operations, and web serving
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
